```

Or use the attached `mrp.zip`:

```
godot --headless --path mrp/ Node.tscn
```

title: Expression can't parse constants and Lua-style syntax
author: MikeSchulze
body:
### Tested versions

v4.7.stable.official [5b4e0cb0f]

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

I want to use the `Expression` to reconstruct values from a string representation.
According to the documentation, it can be composed of any arithmetic operations.
For example, I can use vector arithmetic. `"Vector2(1,1) + Vector2(2,4)"` -> `Vector2(3.0, 5.0)` but it fails when i use constants e.g. `"Vector2.ONE + Vector2(2,4)"`
error: `Error parse expression: 'Vector2.ONE + Vector2(2,4)' Invalid parameter`

The expression can also be used to rebuild dictionaries:
"{"foo": 42}" works  but using lua-style syntax "{foo = 42}" fails
`Error parse expression: '{foo = 42}' Invalid parameter`



### Steps to reproduce

Run this script to see the errors

```
extends Node


func _ready() -> void:
	# Dictionary
	run_expression('{"foo": 42}') # Success
	# Dictionary Lua-style syntax
	run_expression('{foo = 42}') # Fails


	# using constructors
	run_expression('Vector2(1,1)') # Success
	# Using constants
	run_expression('Vector2.ONE') # Fails


func run_expression(expression: String) -> void:
	var e := Expression.new()

	var err := e.parse(expression)
	if err != OK:
		prints("Error parse expression: '%s'" % expression, error_string(err))
		return

	var data :Variant = e.execute([])
	prints( "'%s'" % expression, "==>", data)
```

title: Using Inspectors `Save (as)` on `VoxelGIData` prompts it to be saved as `.tres` or `.tscn` rather than `.res`
author: Arecher
body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible: 4.7.stable ### System information Godot v4.7.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon R7 Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 27.20.20913.2000) - AMD A12-9720P RADEON R7, 12 COMPUTE CORES 4C+8G (4 threads) - 7.46 GiB memory - WASAPI (44100 Hz, Stereo/mono) ### Issue description The default way to store `VoxelGIData` files is in `.res` rather than `.tres` files, so much so, that Godot gives a popup message on every save when the data is saved as a `.tres` instead. When using the `Bake VoxelGI` button on a `VoxelGI` node, the default extension it gives for storing the data is a `.res` file. Using the `Save As` context on the VoxelGIData then attempts to save it under the same name and extension. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Add a VoxelGI node. 2. Bake the VoxelGI node, and store the data as a .res file. 3. Open the VoxelGI node in the inspector 4. Right-click the data 5. Save As OR Make Unique > Save 6. The suggested extension for the VoxelGIData is now .tres If you open a different scene, and Save As the scene under a different name and leave it opened before going through these steps (and use Save As to store the VoxelGIData), you will likely get the issue where it attempts to save it as a .tscn instead. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: Method-chain RefCounted temporaries are not released at end of statement in functions with an Array[String] = [] default argument
author: u-fs
body:
### Tested versions

Reproduced
- v4.7.stable.official.5b4e0cb0f
- v4.5.1.stable.official.f62fdbde1
- v4.3.stable.official.77dcf97d8
- v4.3.rc1.official.e343dbbcc

In versions prior to v4.3.beta3, the editor crashed when opening the MRP, so I was unable to verify the reproduction.

### System information

Godot v4.7.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce MX550 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9608) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1270P (16 threads) - 31.24 GiB memory - WASAPI (48000 Hz, Stereo/mono)

### Issue description

I confirmed that inside a function with an `Array[String] = []` argument, a `RefCounted` object created within a method chain and not stored in a variable is not released at the end of the statement; its release is deferred until the function returns. This occurs even when the argument is explicitly passed.

In the MRP's `main.gd`, `test1()` (taking an untyped `Array = []` as an argument) and `test2()` (taking an `Array[String] = []` as an argument) execute the same statements; by observing the output order of the `print` statement triggered by `NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE` versus the "end of statement" print on the next line, you can verify the timing of the object's deallocation.

```text
test1 (untyped Array default)
    TestRefCounted released      <- released at the end of the statement.
    end of statement
test2 (typed Array[String] default)
    end of statement
    TestRefCounted released      <- not released until the end of the function.
```

According to the comment in `GDScriptByteCodeGenerator::pop_temporary()` ([gdscript_byte_codegen.cpp#L139-L143](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c089e9bf0b8712d0bc444c2ceaf9c543ed9c777/modules/gdscript/gdscript_byte_codegen.cpp#L139-L143)), "the cleanup will be performed an the end of the statement, to allow object references to survive chaining", so my understanding is that being cleaned up at the end of the statement is the intended behavior.

A concrete example of the issue is that a file handle remains open after a chained call like `FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.READ).get_length()`, causing the immediately following `DirAccess.rename_absolute()` to fail on Windows (this can be reproduced using `fileaccess_check.tscn` in the MRP).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the attached MRP in Godot 4.7.
2. Run `main.tscn`.
3. Check the console output. In `test1`, `TestRefCounted released` is output **before** `end of statement` (meaning it is released at the end of the statement), whereas in `test2`, it is output **after**. The expected behavior is for `test2` to follow the same order as `test1`.
4. (Windows only) Run `fileaccess_check.tscn`. Inside a function taking an `Array[String] = []` argument, `FileAccess` is not released at the end of the statement and the file remains open, causing the subsequent `DirAccess.rename_absolute()` to fail.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[refcounted-release-timing-typed-array-default-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29954501/refcounted-release-timing-typed-array-default-mrp.zip)

title: `DirAccess::create_temp` function fails when `prefix` parameter is an empty string
author: ky-bean
body:
### Tested versions

v4.7.stable.official [5b4e0cb0f]

### System information

Godot v4.7.stable - cpe:/o:nixos:nixos:26.11 #1-NixOS ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Tue Jan  1 00:00:00 UTC 1980 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT (RADV NAVI31) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 30.43 GiB memory - PulseAudio (44100 Hz, Stereo/mono)

### Issue description

Calling `DirAccess::create_temp` with default arguments (i.e. the first parameter `prefix` is the empty string `""`) will cause the function to fail with the error:
```
E 0:00:00:382   create_temp_error_demo.gd:4 @ _ready(): Error while creating temporary directory: "" is not a valid prefix.
  <C++ Error>   Method/function failed. Returning: Ref<DirAccess>()
  <C++ Source>  core/io/dir_access.cpp:335 @ create_temp()
  <Stack Trace> create_temp_error_demo.gd:4 @ _ready()

```

[The docs](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_diraccess.html#class-diraccess-method-create-temp) imply that an empty prefix should be an acceptable parameter, and it is the default if it is not explicitly set. Comparing the [`DirAccess::create_temp`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c089e9bf0b8712d0bc444c2ceaf9c543ed9c777/core/io/dir_access.cpp#L333) implementation to the [`FileAccess::create_temp`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c089e9bf0b8712d0bc444c2ceaf9c543ed9c777/core/io/file_access.cpp#L86) yields an obvious difference in the first if-check, where `FileAccess`'s implementation ensures that the prefix is *not* empty before failing but `DirAccess` does not perform that check. As an empty string is [not considered a valid filename](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c089e9bf0b8712d0bc444c2ceaf9c543ed9c777/core/string/ustring.cpp#L4932), this causes `DirAccess::create_temp` to fail when an explicit `prefix` is not provided.

### Steps to reproduce

Write code in a runnable place that calls `DirAccess::create_temp` with `prefix` set to the empty string `""` such as:
```gdscript
func _ready():
	var _temp = DirAccess.create_temp()
```
Run Project
Observe error in Debugger -> Errors

title: GPUParticle Trails `RibbonTrailMesh` doesn't update correctly until the scene is reloaded
author: GustJc
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.7.stable.mono.official [5b4e0cb0f]

### System information

Godot v4.7.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 17134) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.3141) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7700 CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads) - 23.92 GiB memory - WASAPI (96000 Hz, Stereo/mono)

### Issue description

Changing the **Section** and **Section Length** of a DrawPass material `TubeTrailMesh` or `RibbonTrailMesh` completely breaks the particle trail render display. Godot does not update the skinned mesh after those values are changed, breaking the trail renderer.

It only updates it properly after you reload the scene.
Re-starting the emission or toggling emitting does not work.
Only a full reload or resetting the draw pass mesh does it.

The same also happens for **TubeTrailMesh**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2e8d6af6-7f8e-4455-9c90-0aca915b4e37

Here it is with the `RibbonTrailMesh`

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3bde1f0-fd2e-4cca-a849-4dc241c621dd

Alternatively, you can copy the material, remove it, paste it, then toggle the trail to force godot to update properly without a scene reload

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/80e440d3-a46f-4439-acbe-d6ade8e218e6

-----

It seems updating the sections inside the trail mesh requires the GPU particle to update its skinned mesh/skin bind pose to work properly.
But that only happens when the drawpass material is initially set, not when the properties are updated.

I initially though the trail_time was broken because increasing it made the trails blocky no matter how many sections it had (I was ignoring the display bug and trying to understand the trail time at first)

Later I found out, that it was because the sections were not being properly updated until I saved and reloaded the scene.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a GPUParticle3D
- Add a Process material, enable trails
- Create a Drawpass with `RibbonTrailMesh`, create a material with trail enabled
- Change the trail time to 1.0 to see the effect better
- Change the ribbonTrailMesh Section from 5 to 10 and the length from 0.2 to 0.1

**Results:** Broken trails with a lot of artifacts
**Expected:** Trail mesh updated properly, more smooths trails due to higher sections.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Bad Trail Update (MRP).zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29947028/Bad.Trail.Update.MRP.zip)

Select the Tube GPU particle and change the draw pass mesh sections to a higher value to see the bug.

### Tested versions

### System information

```extends Node3D


# Called when the node enters the scene tree for the first time.

	var pre_prompt = """
		```
- "C:/Users/abc/Downloads" (Aliases: Downloads) C:\Users\abc\OneDrive\Code		
		"""
	pass # Replace with function body.

	pass
```

```
- "C:/Users/abc/Downloads" (Aliases: Downloads) C:\Users\abc\OneDrive\Code
